codeReview.summary: The document-readiness-module frontend is implemented but makes API calls to backend endpoints that do not exist. Critical functionality (document upload, delete, self-assessment, reminders) will fail at runtime with 404 errors.

issues.description: uploadDocument() calls POST /documents/upload but this endpoint does not exist in the backend router (apps/api/internal/documents/router.py). The frontend upload feature will fail with 404.
deleteDocument() calls DELETE /documents/{documentId} but this endpoint does not exist in the backend router. Document deletion will fail.
getMedicalSelfAssessment() calls GET /documents/medical/self-assessment but no such endpoint exists. Self-assessment questions will not load.
submitSelfAssessment() calls POST /documents/medical/self-assessment but no such endpoint exists. Self-assessment submission will fail.
setReminder() calls POST /documents/reminders but no such endpoint exists. Setting reminders will fail.
getMyReminders() calls GET /documents/reminders/me but no such endpoint exists. Reminders will not load.
deleteReminder() calls DELETE /documents/reminders/{reminderId} but no such endpoint exists. Deleting reminders will fail.

Missing backend implementation for document upload, delete, self-assessment, and reminder endpoints. The router.py only implements read-only endpoints (checklists, medical guidelines). All write operations (upload, delete, reminders, self-assessment) are missing.
No database models exist for MedicalSelfAssessment (self-assessment questions) or Reminder entities. Even if endpoints were added, there would be no tables to store/query the data.
get_medical_guidelines() returns hardcoded static data instead of querying a medical_guidelines database table. This works for now but is not a proper implementation.
issues.suggestion: Create a MedicalGuideline database model and query it instead of returning static data.

issues.description: The frontend DocumentStage type uses 'new_application' | 'admit_card_released' | 'dv_scheduled' but the backend DocumentStage enum uses 'pst' | 'pet' | 'medical' | 'document_verification'. This mismatch means stage filtering in getDocumentChecklists(currentStage) will not work correctly.
issues.suggestion: Align frontend stage values with backend enum values, or implement stage mapping/translation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker implemented frontend API calls without verifying backend endpoints exist. This is a common pattern violation - frontend and backend should be implemented together or contracts should be agreed upon upfront.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: apps/web/src/lib/api/documents.ts calls POST /documents/upload, DELETE /documents/{id}, POST /documents/medical/self-assessment, etc. - none of which exist in apps/api/internal/documents/router.py

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md defines the backend modules structure including 'documents' under internal/, but the document module was implemented without the upload, delete, self-assessment, and reminder functionality that the feature description requires.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md states 'Documents: Document checklists' but the feature description requires 'upload with validation, reminders, medical self-assessment questionnaire' which need additional endpoints beyond simple checklists.

summary: The document-readiness-module feature fails review due to missing backend API endpoints. The frontend correctly implements the UI (checklist tabs, file upload validation, medical guidelines display, self-assessment questionnaire, reminder modal) but all write operations (upload, delete, reminders, self-assessment) call API endpoints that do not exist in the backend. Additionally, no database models exist for self-assessment questions or reminders. The feature cannot function at runtime without implementing these missing backend components.
